text.title: focus-groups Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for focus-groups as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Dominant participant drives the transcript and LLM analysis treats their views as group consensus.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without diarization and speaker-filtered analysis, a participant generating 50% of transcript volume gets 50% weight in theme extraction. Always diarize and compute unique-speaker-count per theme before any consensus claims.

failure-mode.description: Online groups analyzed without adjusting for the ~30% nonverbal signal loss versus in-person sessions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Zoom groups flatten energy, disagreement, and hesitation. Online findings should be framed as stated attitudes only, not as a proxy for in-person dynamics. Use smaller groups (4-6) and more structured activities to compensate.

failure-mode.description: LLM theme extraction over-clusters nuanced disagreement into a single theme without explicit dissent prompting.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs trained on consensus-seeking corpora produce consensus-seeking summaries. Force dissent extraction with a separate pass: "What disagreements or tensions existed?" before merging with the consensus pass.

failure-mode.description: Auto-generated themes form neat categories without an unclassified bucket.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Every real focus group transcript contains messy, boundary-spanning material that resists clean categorization. Forcing 100% classification loses the material that often drives the most interesting insights. Always include a "messy/unclassified" bucket.

failure-mode.description: LLM-generated discussion guides over-rely on closed-ended probing questions.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents produce grammatically correct but logically leading questions by default. Human researchers must rewrite at least 30% of probes for genuine openness before the guide is used.
